tasks.title: Migrate ablation_c configs to orchestration: block
acceptance_criteria: All 9 ablation_c configs have orchestration: block with strategy + tier_id
Strategy mapping applied: zero_shot→simple_rag_zero, few_shot→simple_rag_few, cot_self→simple_rag_cot
Old answer_generation: block removed from all 9 ablation_c configs
Two baseline configs have no orchestration: block (answer gen disabled by absence)

tasks.title: Wire run_eval.py answer generation to workflow registry
acceptance_criteria: Answer generation block reads orchestration: YAML section instead of answer_generation:
Uses get_workflow(strategy, index=index, llm=llm, retrieval_config=ret_config).invoke()
LLM built from orchestration.tier_id via get_llm()
generated_answers populated from result['answer_text']
retrieved_cache populated from result['docs'] (list[Doc])
Judge context_passages built from docs directly, not corpus_map lookup
No import of harness.answer_gen remains in run_eval.py
Configs without orchestration: block skip answer generation silently

tasks.title: Delete answer_gen.py and update judge integration
acceptance_criteria: harness/answer_gen.py deleted
Judge section uses doc.page_content for context_passages (not corpus_map lookup)
Judge section uses doc.metadata['qa_id'] for cited_qa_ids
Tests for answer_gen.py removed or migrated to workflow tests
No broken imports anywhere

tasks.title: Confirm baselines A0 and A0+ reproduce through workflow path
acceptance_criteria: pytest tests/ passes
python -m harness.run_eval --config harness/configs/ablation_c_mid_zero.yaml succeeds
run_summary.md written under results/ablation_c_mid_zero/
Judge scores match pre-refactor values within ±0.05 (or difference explained)
DECISIONS.md updated with new orchestration: schema entry

tasks.title: Restructure models.yaml into models: + roles: sections
acceptance_criteria: models.yaml has top-level models: dict and roles: dict
models: contains claude_haiku, claude_opus, olmo_32b, local_qwen32 (openai_compatible provider)
roles: contains agent, grader, rewriter, reranker, judge, reviewer
ModelConfig dataclass extended with api_base and api_key_env fields for openai_compatible
load_tier() renamed to load_model_for_role() accepting a role name
Old tiers: section removed
DECISIONS.md updated with role/model separation entry

tasks.title: Refactor harness/llms.py — get_llm(role) API; break tier_id
acceptance_criteria: get_llm(role_name) accepts role strings: 'agent', 'grader', 'rewriter', 'reranker', 'judge', 'reviewer'
openai_compatible provider implemented via llama-index-llms-openai with api_base override
tier_id parameter removed entirely — no backward compat alias
EnvironmentError raised if API key env var is missing for the resolved model
get_llm('agent') returns Haiku by default (per models.yaml roles.agent: claude_haiku)

tasks.title: Migrate all tier_id call sites to get_llm(role)
acceptance_criteria: run_eval.py: orchestration.tier_id removed; llm built as get_llm('agent')
judge.py: constructs LLM via get_llm('judge'), no direct anthropic SDK client
a3_reranker.py and a4_reranker.py: use get_llm('reranker'), no direct SDK
registry.py: get_workflow() no longer accepts tier_id kwarg
All ablation_c configs: tier_id removed from orchestration: block
No reference to TierId, TIER_MID, TIER_FRONTIER, TIER_OLMO in hot paths
pytest tests/ passes
run_eval.py smoke test succeeds

tasks.title: Fix CRAG grader: per-doc JSON scoring + missing_facts + grounded rewrite
acceptance_criteria: _GRADE_SYSTEM replaced with per-doc 0/1/2 scoring prompt returning JSON {per_doc, missing_facts}
grade step returns GradeEvent if any doc scores 2 AND missing_facts is empty
grade step returns InsufficientEvent with missing_facts list when retrieval insufficient
InsufficientEvent carries missing_facts; rewrite step uses them in the rewrite prompt
Rewrite prompt targets specific missing_facts, not just 'try again'
Max-cycles fallback logs the per_doc scores and missing_facts for debugging
graded_docs field in GradeEvent carries per-doc scores as span-annotatable data
Unit tests cover the new grade logic (sufficient/insufficient/max-cycles paths)

tasks.title: Build harness/workflows/react_native.py — hand-written ReAct with per-step events
acceptance_criteria: ReActNativeWorkflow implements StartEvent → think → ThoughtEvent → act → ActionEvent | FinishEvent → observe → ObservationEvent → think loop
Each step is a separate @step producing its own Phoenix span
Same 4 tools as existing react.py: ema_search, get_qa_by_id, list_topics, get_related
FinishEvent carries cited_qa_ids parsed from the agent's final answer
Max iterations guard (default 5) prevents infinite loops
react_native answers the same benchmark questions as react_legacy within ±10% correctness (informal check)
Unit tests cover the thought→act→observe cycle and the max-iter guard

tasks.title: Register react_native; rename legacy react entry in registry
acceptance_criteria: Registry key 'react' now points to react_native builder
Registry key 'react_legacy' points to the old FunctionAgent-based builder
react_review composite updated to use react (native) by default
list_workflows() returns both 'react' and 'react_legacy'
DECISIONS.md updated with react_native decision entry

tasks.title: Document Phoenix annotation setup in docs/SETUP.md
acceptance_criteria: SETUP.md section 'Phoenix annotation schemas' describes how to create step_quality and answer_quality configs in Phoenix UI
step_quality: categorical {good, suboptimal, wrong} — for tool-call and thought spans
answer_quality: 1-5 scale + freeform reason — for root spans
Section 'Phoenix hosting' documents home PC always-on + Elitebook via Tailscale setup
Section notes that annotation queues per strategy (one for 'recent CRAG', one for 'recent react') should be created manually

tasks.title: Create harness/hitl/ package skeleton
acceptance_criteria: harness/hitl/ directory exists with __init__.py
Package docstring explains: HITL code lives here; writes JSONL to Nextcloud; never stores data in-repo
pytest tests/ still passes (no import errors from new package)

tasks.title: Write harness/hitl/export_annotations.py — Phoenix → Nextcloud JSONL
acceptance_criteria: Script queries Phoenix annotations API for labelled spans since a given date
Output JSONL schema: {trace_id, span_id, span_name, input, output, labels, reason, annotated_by, annotated_at}
Writes to ~/Nextcloud/Datasets/ema_nlp/annotations/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl
Supports --since DATE and --strategy STRATEGY_NAME filter flags
Dry-run mode (--dry-run) prints rows to stdout without writing
Handles Phoenix API errors gracefully (logs, exits non-zero)
Unit test with mocked Phoenix client verifies schema and file naming

tasks.title: First SME labelling session + runtime interrupt decision
acceptance_criteria: 5 benchmark questions run through simple_rag_zero, crag, react_native
All spans labelled in Phoenix with step_quality and answer_quality
export_annotations.py successfully exports to Nextcloud JSONL
OPEN_QUESTIONS.md entry for 'runtime HITL interrupt' updated with decision based on labelling UX
If interrupt decided: REFACT-014b added to plan for CRAGReviewWorkflow HumanConfirmEvent

tasks.title: Cache BM25 retriever per session in app.py and run_eval.py
acceptance_criteria: make_bm25_retriever called once per session in app.py on_chat_start
make_bm25_retriever called once in run_eval.py setup phase, passed to retrieve_fn
Subsequent calls within a session reuse the cached instance
No functional change to retrieval results

tasks.title: Drop Doc dataclass; use TextNode end-to-end in workflows and app.py
acceptance_criteria: Doc dataclass removed from workflows/utils.py
results_to_docs() returns list[TextNode]
format_docs() accepts list[TextNode] and reads node.text + node.metadata
app.py iteration over docs uses node.text / node.metadata directly — no hasattr checks
All workflow StopEvent results contain docs: list[TextNode]
run_eval.py judge section updated to use TextNode.text for context_passages
pytest tests/ passes

tasks.title: Promote A3/A4 rerankers to LlamaIndex NodePostprocessor
acceptance_criteria: a3_reranker and a4_reranker implement BaseNodePostprocessor interface
postprocess_nodes() accepts list[NodeWithScore] and returns reranked list[NodeWithScore]
run_eval.py chains postprocessor after base retriever via RetrieverQueryEngine or manual call

Results traceable in Phoenix as a distinct span
Retrieval eval results unchanged for runs without reranking
